scenario_description: A black dog named Shadow is initially indistinguishable from a dark leather armchair, then reveals itself by moving its tail, and finally runs onto a bright rug. The user tracks this entity across three visual contexts to resolve an ambiguous reference.

turns.text: Here's a photo of my living room corner. Is that lump on the chair just a cushion?
turns.image_description: A dimly lit living room corner featuring a high-back armchair upholstered in deep black leather. On the seat of the chair, there is a large, irregular dark shape that perfectly matches the texture and color of the leather upholstery. It appears to be a folded throw blanket or a sagging part of the cushion. No distinct animal features are visible; the shape blends seamlessly into the furniture.

turns.text: It looks like it could be a cushion or a blanket at first glance, but the lighting makes it hard to tell for sure. What do you think?
Wait, look at the second shot. That thing moved!
turns.image_description: The same black leather armchair in the same dim lighting. The dark shape previously seen on the seat has shifted slightly. A fluffy black tail is now clearly visible extending off the edge of the chair, twitching. The body remains mostly hidden against the black leather, but the movement confirms it is a living creature, specifically a black dog.

turns.text: Oh wow, definitely not a cushion then! That tail gives it away. Looks like a black dog was hiding there.
Yeah, he ran out onto the rug. Here is where he ended up.
turns.image_description: A wide shot of the living room showing a large, bright yellow geometric patterned rug covering most of the floor. A medium-sized black dog (Shadow) is standing in the center of the rug, looking towards the camera. In the background, the black leather armchair is visible and empty. To the left of the rug, near a white sofa, sits a small black stuffed bear toy that looks similar in color to the dog but is stationary and much smaller.

turns.text: He made quite a splash on that yellow rug! Much easier to see him there than on the black chair.

Looking back at the first image, I thought that 'lump' was just part of the furniture. Was the object I identified as 'part of the chair' actually the dog? A) Yes, it was the dog camouflaged on the chair. B) No, it was a separate black cushion that fell off. C) No, it was the stuffed bear sitting on the chair.

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 shows a dark shape on a black leather chair that looks like furniture/cushion. 2. Turn 2 shows the same shape moving and revealing a tail, confirming it is a black dog. 3. Turn 3 shows the black dog on a rug and the chair empty, while also introducing a distractor (a small black stuffed bear) elsewhere in the room. 4. The question asks if the 'lump' from Turn 1 was the dog. Since Turn 2 proves the lump moved and had a tail, and Turn 3 shows the dog came from that location, the answer is Yes (Option A). Option B is incorrect because the object moved and had a tail, unlike a cushion. Option C is incorrect because the stuffed bear is seen in Turn 3 on the floor near the sofa, not on the chair, and is too small to match the size of the lump in Turn 1.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the identity of the 'dark lump' across turns: recognizing it as the dog in Turn 2 despite the camouflage in Turn 1, and distinguishing it from the visually similar but spatially distinct stuffed bear introduced in Turn 3. A single-turn analysis of Turn 1 would suggest 'cushion', while analyzing Turn 3 alone might confuse the dog with the bear without the context of the movement in Turn 2.

_meta.scenario: A black dog named Shadow blends perfectly into a dark leather armchair in turn one, appearing as part of the furniture until it moves its tail in turn two, then runs to a bright rug in turn three. The user asks if the object identified as 'part of the chair' in the first image was actually the dog.
